title: Oil Depots Hit in Iran: People Share Their Stories
summary: In Iran, some oil depots were hit by strikes overnight, making the night sky very bright like daytime. People in cities like Tehran and Karaj saw big fires and explosions. They felt scared and confused because they didn't know what was happening. One person said they saw a red light and then a big cloud. Another said the smoke covered the city and they couldn't see the sun. The strikes are part of attacks by the US and Israel on Iran, which started after Iran's leader was killed. Iran has also attacked back. Authorities are checking the air quality and telling people to stay home for safety. The BBC talked to Iranians but kept their names secret to protect them.

keywords.explanation: attacks or hits from bombs or missiles
gray or black cloud from burning things
big, loud bursts that cause damage

questions.question: What happened to the oil depots in Iran?
options: They were moved to another city

background_read: This article is about attacks on oil depots in Iran, which are places where fuel is kept. Strikes are when bombs or missiles hit something, causing fires and explosions. People in Iran are sharing their experiences of seeing bright lights and smoke. The attacks are part of a conflict between Iran and other countries like the US and Israel. Authorities are worried about air quality because smoke can be harmful. The BBC is reporting this to tell people what's happening while keeping the Iranians' names safe.
Article_Structure: WHO: Iranians and authorities in Iran
WHAT: Oil depots were hit by strikes, causing fires and explosions
WHERE: In cities like Tehran and Karaj in Iran
WHY: Because of attacks by the US and Israel, and to report on people's safety concerns

perspectives.description: People in Iran are scared and confused by the bright lights and smoke from the strikes.
